ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to nacho cheese sauce; A00LC (Processed cheese products) used as the closest defensible parent match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for nacho cheese sauce as a distinct food concept; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of nacho cheese sauce as a dipping condiment is approximately 60 g (about 4 tablespoons), as commonly portioned in fast-food and casual dining settings in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cheese sauce, ready-to-serve — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 01019 adapted) and USDA Branded Food reference for processed nacho cheese sauce
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nacho cheese sauce is not listed as a distinct entry in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Cheese sauce, ready-to-serve' (FDC ID 170851) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for commercial nacho cheese sauce products. Values represent a composite estimate for a standard commercial nacho cheese sauce at approximately 30–35% cheese solids content.

gi_source.title: Cheese sauce / processed cheese product — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for processed cheese and dairy-based sauces
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for nacho cheese sauce exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 35 is estimated by analogy with processed cheese and cheese-based dairy products, which consistently fall in the low-GI range (GI 20–40) due to high fat and protein content slowing gastric emptying and glucose absorption. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 6.48) / 100 ≈ 2.3. Carbohydrate per 60 g serving = 10.8 × 0.60 = 6.48 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (620 mg per 100 g; ~372 mg per 60 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit nacho cheese sauce due to its high sodium content of approximately 620 mg per 100 g, which can contribute meaningfully to the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; portion control to no more than 30 g per occasion is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate content with low glycemic index; high saturated fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Nacho cheese sauce has a low estimated glycemic index (~35) and moderate carbohydrate content (~10.8 g per 100 g), making acute glycaemic impact manageable in small portions; however, its high saturated fat content (5.5 g per 100 g) may contribute to insulin resistance with regular consumption, and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should use it sparingly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (5.5 g/100 g), trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), sodium (620 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (28 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Nacho cheese sauce presents a high cardiovascular concern due to its combination of elevated saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and cholesterol; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should treat it as an occasional condiment and limit serving size to no more than 30 g to minimise atherogenic and hypertensive risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nacho cheese sauce is a smooth, pourable processed cheese condiment of American Tex-Mex origin, widely served as a dipping sauce for tortilla chips (nachos), chicken wings, and other fried snacks. It is made primarily from processed cheddar cheese blended with water, modified starch, vegetable oil, emulsifying salts (such as sodium phosphate), and natural flavourings, then heated to produce a uniform, viscous, bright-orange sauce. The texture is creamy and glossy, with a salty, tangy, mildly sharp cheese flavour and a characteristic processed-cheese aroma. In Singapore, nacho cheese sauce appears as a side condiment or dipping option at Western fast-food and casual dining restaurants, including Wingstop outlets, where it is paired with chicken wings and fries. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 166 kcal per 100 g, with notable fat content (11.2 g/100 g, of which 5.5 g is saturated), moderate protein (5.8 g/100 g), and high sodium (620 mg/100 g). Its glycemic index is estimated to be low (~35) due to the fat and protein matrix slowing carbohydrate absorption. Clinically, it is of concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium and saturated fat content, and should be consumed in small portions by individuals managing these conditions. It is a source of calcium (~180 mg/100 g) and vitamin B12 (~0.4 mcg/100 g) from its dairy base. It is vegetarian but not vegan, and contains dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in nacho cheese sauce?
Is nacho cheese sauce suitable for diabetics?
Nacho cheese sauce at Wingstop Singapore
Nacho cheese sauce GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: Nacho cheese sauce is not listed as a distinct entry in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 'Cheese sauce, ready-to-serve' and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for commercial nacho cheese sauce products. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for processed cheese and dairy-based sauces. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin C is reported as 0.0 mg per 100 g; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for processed cheese sauce, as ascorbic acid is not present in the formulation — confirmed zero. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin A, B-vitamins, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170851) and are not sentinel values (-1); all are reported numerically from the source. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record. Halal status is marked false as the halal certification of the specific commercial nacho cheese sauce products served at the listed Singapore restaurants cannot be confirmed from approved sources; the cuisine_tags field in the input listed 'Halal' as a tag for the broader restaurant category (Wingstop), but this cannot be assumed to apply to this specific condiment without verified certification. The food_name has been corrected from the generic input 'Cheese' to the more precise canonical name 'Nacho Cheese Sauce' consistent with the input description 'Nacho cheese sauce'. Cuisine has been corrected from 'Fried Chicken' (a restaurant category tag) to 'American / Tex-Mex' reflecting the actual culinary origin of this condiment. Multilingual names are provided for Singaporean context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this food is served in Singapore. FoodEx2 code A00LC (Processed cheese products) is used as a closest_parent match; no nacho-cheese-sauce-specific FoodEx2 code exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue.
